commits: eb7f268: docs(0.5.1-SET-unify): design memo — migration map, R6/R2/AC-0, test plan
4883592: test(0.5.1-SET-unify): RED — budgets/failover via get_settings(), R6/R2/AC-0
424f278: feat(0.5.1-SET-unify): GREEN — budgets/failover via get_settings(); delete hidden defaults
61e8393: test(0.5.1-SET-unify): RED — failover targets must be constrained to model_list catalog
5be1e8e: fix(0.5.1-SET-unify): constrain failover targets to the model_list catalog

review_findings_addressed.finding: check_model_with_filters() returned the first healthy failover candidate without validating it exists in the proxy's model_list; because unknown models are healthy-by-default, a typo'd config fallback or AIRLOCK_FAILOVER_MAP override could reroute to a non-existent LiteLLM alias.
review_findings_addressed.resolution: Added read-only router.known_model_aliases() exposing the alias->provider catalog cached by set_router_config (built from model_list[*].litellm_params.model). The failover loop now skip-and-warns any candidate not in the catalog. Covers BOTH config-fallbacks-derived maps and AIRLOCK_FAILOVER_MAP overrides (the filter sits in the shared candidate loop). SAFE FALLBACK: filtering applies only when the catalog is non-empty (`if catalog and fallback not in catalog`) — an empty/unconfigured catalog disables filtering, preserving prior behavior so failover is never suppressed merely because model_list was not loaded.
review_findings_addressed.catalog_read: circuit_breaker imports the NEW router.known_model_aliases() (returns set(_alias_provider_map)); it already imported infer_provider from router and get_settings from the fast package.

tests.note: Original pack RED was 16 failing across the four target files (7 settings defaults, 3 router no-swap, 5 circuit-breaker config-driven, 1 monitor R6). Codex review-fix RED added 3 more failing failover-catalog tests (commit 61e8393). After the fix: 270 passed across the targeted set (coordinator set tests/test_fast_circuit_breaker.py + tests/test_circuit_breaker.py + tests/test_fast_router.py = 98 passed). Full non-live suite: 2160 passed; the only suite failure is tests/test_fathom_init.py::test_init_engine_with_fathomdb, a PRE-EXISTING unrelated WIP failure (fathomdb not installed; confirmed failing on the clean baseline) and out of scope. 1 pre-existing xpass (TestAliasTableCoverage gemini alias collision).

blockers_encountered.description: airlock/tui/screens/overview.py (out-of-MODIFY) lazily imports circuit_breaker._load_failover_map. The spec said DELETE that loader; a hard delete would silently disable the TUI failover-column display (the import is try/except-guarded so it degrades to no-op rather than crashing).
blockers_encountered.resolution: Kept _load_failover_map as a THIN shim returning get_settings().failover_map (env override + list-of-dicts conversion handled inside get_settings). The stale value-carrying _DEFAULT_FAILOVER_MAP and the env-cached loader body are deleted; the shim keeps the TUI consumer working without touching overview.py. DoD #1 (no hidden value-carrying default) is met.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: Removed monitor.configure_budgets + _configured_budgets and dropped its call+import in proxy.py startup wiring (proxy already calls configure_settings(config) immediately above). _explicit_budget_for kept as a thin get_settings().provider_budgets accessor.
circuit_breaker._load_failover_map kept as a thin get_settings() shim for the TUI overview consumer (see blocker DEP-tui-failover-shim); deleted the stale gpt-4o/gpt-4o-mini default map + env cache.

Adjacent test fixups required by the deletions (all in tests/, in scope): tests/test_circuit_breaker.py rewritten to test the shim (old env-cache tests obsolete); tests/test_config_consistency.py test_default_budgets_cover_tier_providers replaced with a no-hidden-default assertion; tests/test_routing_guardrails.py budget-warn tests migrated off configure_budgets/_configured_budgets onto configure_settings/get_settings; tests/harness/test_phase3_routing.py reads get_settings().provider_budgets; tests/test_fast_guardian.py failover test now supplies AIRLOCK_FAILOVER_MAP (no hidden default).

Updated now-stale docstrings/comments in settings.py (module note, AirlockSettings/get_settings) and circuit_breaker.py module docstring to reflect the removed defaults; simplified AirlockSettings provider_budgets/failover_map field default_factories to plain dict.

Behavior note for orchestrator HITL: the TUI overview failover column is now empty unless the TUI process configures settings (it currently runs get_settings() unconfigured) — consistent with the operator-confirmed no-hidden-defaults decision; display-only, no routing impact.
agent_verify_result: pass — 270 passed across targeted files (incl. regression sweep over fast/ router/monitor/circuit_breaker/settings + guardian + routing_guardrails + config_consistency + phase3 harness); full non-live suite 2160 passed (only the pre-existing unrelated fathom WIP fails); ruff check + format clean on all changed files; smoke import OK (TUI shim resolves, removed symbols absent).
next_step_for_orchestrator: Pack 0.5.1-SET-unify on branch feat/0.5.1-SET-unify at 5be1e8e87b8e2869fd43fbac9be8b432b5bcd2dd; codex medium finding (failover-not-constrained-to-model_list) addressed (see review_findings_addressed). Review (esp. HITL on the operator-confirmed behavior change: no config budget => no swap/warn, the TUI failover-display note + the kept _load_failover_map shim), verify, merge. NOTE: now that failover targets are filtered against model_list, any config.yaml `fallbacks` alias not present in `model_list` will be skipped — confirm the shipped fallbacks all resolve to real model_list aliases. Warn-ratio collapse remains for SET-warnratio; state.py untouched.
